system_prompt:
You are distilling one chapter of a guitar method book into a short summary.

The user will give you a chapter file with topic-organized verbatim quotes
plus the human-rationale notes for each. Write a one-paragraph summary (4-7
sentences) that captures:

  - what this chapter teaches (the techniques, concepts, rules, examples)
  - the master's characteristic moves visible in this chapter (if any)
  - the load-bearing terminology this chapter introduces

Reference the topic labels from the chapter file when useful. Do not invent
information not present in the chapter. If the chapter is biographical or
introductory rather than instructional, say so explicitly.

Output just the paragraph — no preamble, no JSON, no markdown formatting.
user_prompt:
Chapter 8: Appendix 1 - Chord Dictionaries

<<<CHAPTER FILE
# Chapter 8 — Appendix 1 - Chord Dictionaries

_Pages 264-285._

> **Verbatim-quote constraint.** The quotes below are exact substrings of the raw transcript produced by `pdftotext`. The transcript may contain extraction artifacts where the book's typeface confuses the tool — e.g. `7irst` or `<irst` for `first`, `speci7ic` for `specific`. **Do NOT manually fix these in this file**: the quote-fidelity validator compares against the raw transcript, so a 'fixed' quote will fail verification and break Stage 2 idempotency. If a future pipeline iteration adds artifact-normalization, it must normalize the transcript and these files together.

## Chord melody dictionary purpose

> This page features a handy reference of all the chord voicings that comprise the chord dictionary
> covered in Module 3.

**Significance (p. 265).** States the role of the chord-melody dictionary as a consolidated reference for the Module 3 voicings.

## Major II V I string set 4321

> MAJOR II V I CHORDS – STRING SET 4321

**Significance (p. 266).** Names a load-bearing voicing family in O'Rourke's method: major ii V I voicings on the top four strings.

## Major II V I string set 5432

> MAJOR II V I CHORDS – STRING SET 5432

**Significance (p. 269).** Names the second canonical string set for major ii V I voicings, completing the pair.

## Minor II V I string set 4321

> MINOR II V I CHORDS – STRING SET 4321

**Significance (p. 272).** Names the minor-key counterpart string set, including the b5 and b9 alterations characteristic of minor ii V i.

## Minor II V I string set 5432

> MINOR II V I CHORDS – STRING SET 5432

**Significance (p. 275).** Names the second minor-key string set, completing the four-way taxonomy of ii V i voicings in this method.

## Tensions and extensions on dominants

> TENSIONS & EXTENSIONS ON DOMINANT CHORDS

**Significance (p. 278).** Heads the dictionary section covering altered/extended dominants, a load-bearing color resource for the method.

## Solo guitar shell voicings

> Here’s an overview of useful solo guitar voicings as featured in module 4.

**Significance (p. 280).** Frames the second dictionary section as the canonical solo-guitar voicing reference for Module 4.

## Shell voicings root on 6th

> SHELL VOICINGS
>
> Root on the 6th String

**Significance (p. 280).** Names the 6th-string-root shell-voicing family, the structural backbone of O'Rourke's solo-guitar comping.

## Shell voicing extensions catalog

> SHELL VOICING EXTENSIONS

**Significance (p. 281).** Heads the catalog of extensions added on top of shells, the load-bearing harmonic vocabulary for solo arranging.

CHAPTER FILE>>>
